title: Text Preprocessing Path
description: Build fluency in Text Preprocessing inside Natural Language Processing by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Tokenization
steps.summary: The process of breaking down text into individual words or tokens. It sits inside Natural Language Processing / Text Preprocessing. Compare it with Tokenizer and Token Masking. It opens into Action Tokenization and Subword Tokenization.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Token Masking
steps.summary: Token Masking belongs to the Natural Language Processing area of AI/ML, within Text Preprocessing. It sits inside Natural Language Processing / Text Preprocessing. Start after Token and Masking. It opens into Frequency-based Token Masking.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Text Normalization
steps.summary: Text Normalization belongs to the Natural Language Processing area of AI/ML, within Text Preprocessing. It sits inside Natural Language Processing / Text Preprocessing. Start after Text-to-Text Models and Normalization.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Lemmatization and stemming
steps.summary: Lemmatization and stemming belongs to the Natural Language Processing area of AI/ML, within Text Preprocessing. It sits inside Natural Language Processing / Text Preprocessing. Start after Stemming and Lemmatization.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Tokenizer
steps.summary: Tokenizer belongs to the Natural Language Processing area of AI/ML, within Text Preprocessing. It sits inside Natural Language Processing / Text Preprocessing. Compare it with Tokenization and Token Masking. It opens into Unigram Language Model Tokenizer.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
